notes: The original SVG includes the gray/white checkerboard as real paths.
The clean source keeps colored and dark mascot paths only, preserving source order.

parts.role: central head/body mass plus merged lower limbs
parts.animation: Use subtle whole-body sway only. This path is merged with tentacle fills, so do not rotate it aggressively.

parts.role: sleepy eyes, brows, pupils, and smile
parts.animation: Blink by compressing the eye whites/pupils vertically under the existing brow line. Preserve the half-lidded expression.

parts.role: purple ribbed dome, dark outline, lavender brim
parts.animation: Small delayed bob or squash, anchored to the head. Keep the brim wide and flat.

parts.role: raised tentacle on viewer-left with two visible suckers
parts.animation: Rotate 3-6 degrees around the base and add tip delay. Keep the inward curl and narrow tip.
Use slow arc motion from the base; preserve the tall asymmetry and hooked top.
Wave from the base with low amplitude. Keep the flattened side-sweep silhouette.
Small outward/inward wave. Do not simplify into a straight tube.

parts.role: viewer-left lower sweep and center-left dangling limb
parts.animation: Separate into masks if possible. Keep tips tapered and irregular.

parts.role: viewer-right lower sweep and center-right dangling limb
parts.animation: Use a slower counter-sway than the left side. Keep the right side visually heavier and taller.

antiRegressionRules: Do not use a dark/glowing background as part of the mascot asset.
Do not add glossy 3D balls or beads; the original has flat oval suckers attached to tentacles.
Do not replace the sleepy half-lidded eyes with round alert eyes.
Do not center or symmetrize the tentacles; the source silhouette is intentionally asymmetric.
Do not redraw from generic octopus primitives unless the result is checked against octo-mate.png.
